deltas.releaseNoteText: ActivityRecord type reference corrected: (id, featureType, actionType, actionUser, timestamp, etc.) → (id, featureType, action, user, timestamp, target, detail)
deltas.summary: The ActivityRecord read-model interface exposes 'action' and 'user' fields, not 'actionType' and 'actionUser' — update the TypeScript interface block to use the corrected field names and add 'target' and 'detail' fields as documented.

details.signature: interface ActivityRecord { id: string; featureType: ActivityFeatureType; action: string; user: User; timestamp: number; target?: ActivityTarget; detail?: unknown; ... }
details.codeExample:
interface ActivityRecord {
  id: string;
  featureType: ActivityFeatureType;   // 'comment' | 'reaction' | 'recorder' | 'crdt' | 'custom'
  action: string;                      // Specific action (use constants from config-action-type-filters rule)
  user: User;                          // User who performed the action
  timestamp: number;                   // Unix timestamp (ms)
  target?: ActivityTarget;             // Entity this log targets
  detail?: unknown;                    // Additional action-specific data
  displayMessage?: string;             // Resolved human-readable message (computed client-side)
  immutable?: boolean;                 // Cannot be updated/deleted if true
}

deltas.ambiguityNote: The docs corrected the prose description of ActivityRecord from listing 'actionType, actionUser' to 'action, user'. The existing skill interface is comprehensive and may reflect internal SDK field names that differ from the wireframe slot variable names. Verify the actual SDK type shape before applying — if 'actionType'/'actionUser' are the correct SDK type fields, this is a no-op. If the SDK type genuinely uses 'action'/'user', update the interface. The 'target' and 'detail' additions are also from the docs correction and should be added if confirmed.

deltas.releaseNoteText: Code examples now use source-verbatim React (camelCase veltIf/veltClass, PascalCase VeltData/VeltIf); docs-invented 'Putting it together' blocks removed. Activity Log wireframe: velt-if → veltIf, velt-class → veltClass in React JSX examples.
deltas.targetSection: Correct (read the slot's injected variables via velt-data / velt-if / velt-class)
deltas.summary: React JSX code blocks in the activity log wireframe-variables rule use the HTML attribute forms 'velt-if' and 'velt-class' instead of the correct JSX prop forms 'veltIf' and 'veltClass'; update the JSX examples to match source-verbatim syntax.

details.codeExample: Show more

deltas.releaseNoteText: Recorder root sections noted as having no direct wireframe slot. Collapsed-button gating corrected: velt-if="{componentConfigSignal.recordingInProgress}" → velt-if="{componentConfigSignal.isRecording}" for the -on/-off variants.
deltas.summary: The collapsed-button-on and collapsed-button-off wireframe variants should be gated on 'componentConfigSignal.isRecording' (not 'recordingInProgress'); the rule's summary table at the collapsed-button row uses 'recordingInProgress' which conflicts with the corrected SDK docs.

deltas.releaseNoteText: Code examples now use source-verbatim React (camelCase veltIf/veltClass, PascalCase VeltData/VeltIf). Recorder wireframe React JSX examples used HTML attribute form 'velt-if'/'velt-class' inside jsx code blocks.
deltas.targetSection: Correct (read the slot's injected variables via velt-data / velt-if / velt-class)
deltas.summary: The 'Correct' JSX code block for VeltRecorderButtonWireframe uses HTML attribute syntax 'velt-class' and 'velt-if' inside a jsx block; update to camelCase 'veltClass' and 'veltIf' to match source-verbatim React syntax.

skipped.reason: Notification type description added 'type', 'icon', 'documentName' fields — but the notifications skill rules reference the Notification type by name only and contain no typed interface block that would be stale. The 'setting.icon' → 'setting.name' fix in the settings accordion trigger icon table is wireframe-slot level content not present in the skill rules. No skill rule content to update.

Live Selection wireframe support declared as limited / not yet available. The single-editor-mode skill is not about live-selection wireframes. No dedicated live-selection skill exists. The docs change is a clarification that wireframe customization is CSS-only for now — no existing skill rule documents a live-selection wireframe pattern to correct.

Root wireframe entry point changed from '<velt-transcription-wireframe>' to mode-specific tags. Checked velt-recorder-best-practices/rules/shared/wireframe-variables/wireframe-variables-recorder-transcription.md — need to verify if the root tag is referenced there. Treat as medium-priority follow-up: if the transcription rule references '<velt-transcription-wireframe>' as the entry point, that would require a delta. Per scope restriction the recorder transcription rule should be audited by the patch applier per delta-003/004 context.

Changes are velt-if→veltIf/velt-class→veltClass syntax corrections in JSX examples and removal of 'Putting it together' invented blocks. The comments skill rules do not contain wireframe-variables rule files with these invented examples — comments skill focuses on modes, UI, data, not wireframe slot template-variable patterns at this level. No stale rule content identified.
Area skill (velt-area-best-practices) exists but the diff is prose/example cleanup only. No API-level changes to area wireframe variables.
Reactions skill (velt-reactions-best-practices) exists. Diff is prose/example cleanup — no type field corrections or behavior changes.
Rewriter skill (velt-rewriter-best-practices) exists. File was deleted entirely (–76 lines net). Diff shows removal of docs-invented content; no new API documented.
View analytics skill (velt-view-analytics-best-practices) exists. File deleted entirely. Diff shows removal of docs-invented content; no new API documented.

Cursors skill (velt-cursors-best-practices) exists. Diff is prose/example cleanup and velt-if→veltIf syntax corrections. Checked — the cursors skill does not contain wireframe-variables rules with JSX code blocks using velt-if. No stale content.
Presence skill (velt-presence-best-practices) exists. Diff is prose/example cleanup. No type field corrections or behavior changes propagated into skill rules.

Cleanup of docs-invented 'Putting it together' blocks. The notifications skill wireframe-variables rule (wireframe-variables-notifications-tool.md) was not authored from these invented examples. No stale content.
